summary: Stellar To Lay Me Down MIDI, tight set and soaring late‑night jams
blurb: The first set rolls from a crisp Touch of Grey into a powerful To Lay Me Down (MIDI solo) and The Music Never Stopped. Set two delivers the classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue, Ship Of Fools into Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude, and closes with an inspired Stella Blue and Sugar Magnolia. Brent’s bright keyboards and Jerry’s soaring tone keep the band locked‑in throughout.
review: April 1, 1990 at The Omni captured one of spring ’90’s most vibrant Dead nights. Across a dozen audience and matrix tapes the consensus is clear: the band was firing on all cylinders. Jerry Garcia’s guitar shone brightest during his daring MIDI solo on “To Lay Me Down” and his luminous phrasing on “Stella Blue,” while Brent Mydland supplied bright, cutting organ work that propelled numbers like “Candyman,” “Walkin’ Blues,” and “Big River.” The setlist flowed effortlessly – the first set built momentum with a tight “Touch of Grey” opening and a soaring “The Music Never Stopped,” then the second set exploded with the beloved “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider” transition, a masterful “Ship Of Fools → Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude” suite, and a climactic “Stella Blue” that segued into a jubilant “Sugar Magnolia.” Reviewers repeatedly praised the flawless Charlie Miller soundboard capture for its crystal‑clear detail, making it the definitive source to hear every nuance of this high‑energy performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All twelve recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly highlighting Jerry’s MIDI solo, Brent’s bright keyboards, and the seamless transitions (China > Rider, Ship Of Fools suite). No negative comments appear, so a strong positive adjustment of +1.6 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Flawless Charlie Miller SBD capture – crystal‑clear, no dropouts, and praised as the most complete representation of the show’s sound.

key_highlights: MIDI solo on To Lay Me Down that electrifies the set
Seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition feeding into a powerful Ship Of Fools suite
Brent Mydland’s bright keyboard textures driving the jams, especially on Candyman and Walkin' Blues
Stella Blue closes the second set with soaring emotion and immaculate tone

band_performance.Jerry: Delivered a sparkling tone, highlighted by his striking MIDI solo on To Lay Me Down and luminous phrasing on Stella Blue, with consistently praised soaring leads throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Provided clear, well‑imaged bass that anchored the groove, especially evident on the high‑fidelity soundboard recordings.
band_performance.Brent: His organ and synth work was bright and cutting, driving the jams—from crisp comping on Candyman to soaring solos on Walkin’ Blues and Big River—making the keyboard a central voice all night.
